_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
March 11th, 6:15 AM
Good morning riders and skiers! The forecast calls for a breezy morning under cloudy skies with gently rising temps and an increasing chance of precipitation. We will be sliding under a slight temperature inversion throughout the day with temps in the low to mid 40's at the base and upper 40's at the summit.  Saddle up your steed of choice and join us for these spring-like conditions while we've got them, as colder temps and more wintery conditions are expected to clock back in by Thursday. With temps staying above freezing last night our ability for grooming was limited. Many trails will remain ungroomed, so expect variable conditions, with a mix of loose and frozen granular this morning. However, surfaces will soften to corn snow as temps climb and see more skier traffic through the day. We'll be getting down on 474 skiable acres with 10 lifts, and 15 of 109 open trails groomed.

GMX will be closed today due to a blown fuse as we work with a third party to fix the issue.  North Ridge Express will be closed today for maintenance as the lift ops crew diligently works to remedy an electrical problem on the tower monitoring circuits with the manufacturer.  We do still plan to open Mt. Ellen today with Inverness and Sunny Q in operation. Summit will be closed as neither GMX nor North Ridge will be spinning. All other scheduled lifts spin at 9:00 AM. Slide Brook Express will be closed for the foreseeable future due to a lack of snow on the evacuation roads.   Inverness will be reserved for race training today. Brambles will be open to the public.

The legendary Sugarbush Parks crew continues to deliver, at Mt. Ellen you’ll find over 80 of the finest features on Riemergasse. At Lincoln Peak, Slowpoke has 18 intermediate features, and Sugarbear Forest is home to 7 beginner freestyle elements. Sugar Run has been stripped of its features, and the upper half will remain open as a groomed trail today in the name of snow preservation. Riemergasse will be closed Wednesday and Thursday for a rebuild and reset for a Friday opening. 

There's plenty of fun to be had both on and off the hill this winter, so check out all our winter events. Get ready for an all-out showdown on the gnarliest terrain Sugarbush has to offer! The Castlerock Extreme is back, March 14th, we're calling on bold skiers and riders to conquer the iconic Lift Line. Think you’ve got what it takes? This is where legends are made.

All Lincoln Peak and Mt. Ellen uphill routes are currently open. Please review our Uphill Travel Policy for full details.
